full_prompt: Design a CFD-and-FEA-coupled small UAV propeller as an open-source multiphysics benchmark, replicating a typical simulation-driven design pattern. The propeller is a two-blade carbon-fiber / epoxy composite propeller of 280 mm diameter (11 inch) for a quadcopter propulsion stack, driven by a brushless DC motor at 8000 rpm (133 Hz), producing a design thrust of 8.0 N per propeller in hover at sea level (air density 1.225 kg/m^3). Material is a quasi-isotropic carbon/epoxy lay-up (E1 = E2 = 70 GPa, tensile strength 600 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3). The propeller must simultaneously satisfy: (R1) max von Mises stress at the blade root under 8000 rpm centrifugal plus aerodynamic pressure from CFD must not exceed 300 MPa (tensile 600 / SF 2.0), (R2) blade tip deflection under combined centrifugal plus aerodynamic load must not exceed 2.0 mm (0.7% of diameter) to preserve blade-to-airframe clearance, (R3) first natural frequency at 0 rpm must be at least 1.5x the 133 Hz operating rotational frequency = 200 Hz to avoid 1P resonance, and the first mode at 8000 rpm (with centrifugal stiffening) must stay clear of 1P and 2P by at least 10%, (R4) CFD steady thrust at 8000 rpm in hover must match 8.0 N +/- 5% (target figure of merit >= 0.7), and (R5) propeller mass must not exceed 15 g per blade or 30 g total for the two-blade prop. Fluid analysis (CFD) is required for R4. Vibration (modal + Campbell) is required for R3. Thermal and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body of the propeller plus a report listing blade-root max von Mises, tip deflection, first natural frequency at 0 and 8000 rpm, CFD hover thrust and figure of merit, and total mass.
short_prompt:
Design a two-blade carbon-fiber / epoxy UAV propeller for a quadcopter propulsion stack: 280 mm (11 inch) diameter, 8 mm hub ID, quasi-isotropic carbon/epoxy laminate, driven at 8000 rpm (133 Hz) by a BLDC motor and producing a design hover thrust of 8.0 N per propeller at sea level (air density 1.225 kg/m^3). Hub is clamped to the motor shaft; blades are free.

Load cases.
- LC1 centrifugal + aero: 8000 rpm centrifugal plus aerodynamic pressure from steady hover CFD.
- LC2 hover CFD target: 8.0 N thrust +/- 5% at 8000 rpm, figure of merit target.
- LC3 modal / Campbell: first mode at 0 rpm above 200 Hz; at 8000 rpm, separation from 1P/2P by at least 10%.

Total mass is a UAV-prop budget constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Blade element momentum theory gives the baseline thrust / torque prior to CFD, used as an initial-guess pre-solver.

prompt.title: 11-inch quadcopter carbon/epoxy propeller
prompt.brief: Design a 280 mm 2-blade carbon/epoxy UAV propeller for 8000 rpm producing 8.0 N hover thrust per prop, passing root stress, tip deflection, modal separation from 1P/2P, CFD thrust accuracy, and 30 g pair mass.

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Thin blade dominated by centrifugal tension, not buckling.
